expectations.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Top-level src/ directly contains auth/, users/, billing/, health/ feature slices, with only thin http/ and lib/ cross-cutting dirs.

expectations.text: layout.txt colocates test files next to source files rather than in a distant tests/ tree
expectations.evidence: Every source file has a sibling .test.ts (e.g., issue-refund.ts and issue-refund.test.ts) within its feature directory.

expectations.text: layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users
expectations.evidence: Layout explicitly includes auth/, users/, and billing/ directories with labeled 'FEATURE SLICE' comments.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — avoiding generic names or requiring domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Section 3 requires domain-specific verbs like issueRefund and bans generic filenames utils.ts, helpers.ts, manager.ts, service.ts, handler.ts.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: Section 2 states 'Hard cap: 300 lines per file. Soft target: 150.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: Section 4 requires 'Typed public signatures everywhere. No any' with strict and noImplicitAny enabled.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Section 8 defines 'bun run check runs bun typecheck && bun lint && bun test' as the agent's feedback loop.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: Section 5 bans Proxy, Reflect, runtime monkey-patching, and decorator stacks because 'Agents can't grep through them.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Rationale repeatedly cited, e.g., 'Files over 300 regularly break agent apply-model merges' and 'Agent memory of an API is version-specific.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 4 specifies 'type UserId = string & { readonly __brand: UserId }' and forbids passing raw string where an ID is meant.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md prefers discriminated unions (or equivalent) over magic-string dispatch / string-keyed registries — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 4 and 5 mandate discriminated unions with exhaustive switch and ban 'magic-string dispatch' / registerHandler tables.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 9 requires exact pinned versions and 'Prefer boring, popular packages' because 'Agent memory of an API is version-specific.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects (clock, fetch, env as arguments) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 8 mandates freezing time via injected clock, seeded randomness, and fixed UUIDs; Section 5 injects deps { db, clock, logger, fetch, env }.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 states 'No barrel files (index.ts that only re-exports). Import from the defining file.'
